comments:
Symmetry — Geometric symmetry primitives for design systems.

## Design Philosophy

Symmetry describes invariance under transformation. A shape has symmetry
when it looks the same after applying certain operations (reflection,
rotation, translation). This module provides:

- **Reflection symmetry**: Mirror across an axis (bilateral)
- **Rotational symmetry**: N-fold rotation about a center
- **Translational symmetry**: Periodic repetition
- **Compound symmetry**: Combinations (dihedral, wallpaper groups)

## Use Cases

- Layout systems (bilateral UI layouts)
- Icon/logo generation (radial, dihedral)
- Pattern/tile generation (wallpaper groups)
- Procedural geometry (snowflakes, mandalas)
- Accessibility (symmetric UI aids comprehension)

## Mathematical Foundation

Symmetries form groups under composition:
- Identity is always a symmetry
- Every symmetry has an inverse
- Composition of symmetries is a symmetry

## Dependencies

- Prelude (Eq, Ord, Show)
- Geometry.Angle (rotation angles)
- Geometry.Point (center points, axis endpoints)

## Dependents

- Schema.Geometry.Shape (symmetric shape construction)
- Schema.Geometry.Transform (symmetry-based transforms)
- Component.ColorPicker (radial symmetry)
- Component.Icon (logo symmetries)

declarations.comments:
Axis of reflection symmetry.

A reflection axis is defined by its angle from horizontal.
- 0° = horizontal axis (reflects top-bottom)
- 90° = vertical axis (reflects left-right)
- 45° = diagonal axis
- 135° = anti-diagonal axis
declarations.title: ReflectionAxis

declarations.comments: Create a reflection axis at the given angle
declarations.title: reflectionAxis

declarations.comments: Horizontal reflection axis (mirrors top-bottom)
declarations.title: horizontalAxis

declarations.comments: Vertical reflection axis (mirrors left-right)
declarations.title: verticalAxis

declarations.comments: Diagonal reflection axis (45°, mirrors across main diagonal)
declarations.title: diagonalAxis

declarations.comments: Anti-diagonal reflection axis (135°, mirrors across anti-diagonal)
declarations.title: antiDiagonalAxis

declarations.comments: Get the angle of a reflection axis
declarations.title: axisAngle

declarations.comments: Check if axis is horizontal (within tolerance)
declarations.title: isHorizontalAxis

declarations.comments: Check if axis is vertical (within tolerance)
declarations.title: isVerticalAxis

declarations.comments: Check if axis is diagonal (45° or 135°)
declarations.title: isDiagonalAxis

declarations.comments: Get the perpendicular axis
declarations.title: perpendicularAxis

declarations.comments:
Rotational symmetry with N-fold rotation.

An N-fold rotational symmetry means the shape looks identical after
rotation by 360°/N. For example:
- 2-fold: looks same after 180° rotation (rectangle)
- 3-fold: looks same after 120° rotation (equilateral triangle)
- 4-fold: looks same after 90° rotation (square)
- ∞-fold: looks same after any rotation (circle)

We use `folds = 0` to represent no rotational symmetry,
and `folds = -1` to represent continuous (circular) symmetry.
declarations.title: RotationalSymmetry

declarations.comments:
Create rotational symmetry with N folds.
Values < 1 are treated as no symmetry, except -1 means circular.
declarations.title: rotationalSymmetry

declarations.comments: No rotational symmetry
declarations.title: noRotationalSymmetry

declarations.comments: 2-fold rotational symmetry (180° rotation)
declarations.title: bilateral

declarations.comments: 3-fold rotational symmetry (120° rotation)
declarations.title: trilateral

declarations.comments: 4-fold rotational symmetry (90° rotation)
declarations.title: quadrilateral

declarations.comments: 5-fold rotational symmetry (72° rotation)
declarations.title: pentagonal

declarations.comments: 6-fold rotational symmetry (60° rotation)
declarations.title: hexagonal

declarations.comments: 8-fold rotational symmetry (45° rotation)
declarations.title: octagonal

declarations.comments: Continuous rotational symmetry (circle)
declarations.title: circular

declarations.comments: Get the number of folds (0 = none, -1 = continuous)
declarations.title: foldCount

declarations.comments: Get the rotation angle for this symmetry
declarations.title: rotationAngle

declarations.comments: Check if this is exactly N-fold symmetry
declarations.title: isNFold

declarations.comments: Check if shape has any rotational symmetry
declarations.title: hasRotationalSymmetry

declarations.comments:
Dihedral symmetry: rotation + reflection combined.

Dihedral group D_n has:
- N-fold rotational symmetry
- N reflection axes

Examples:
- D_3: equilateral triangle (3 rotations, 3 reflections)
- D_4: square (4 rotations, 4 reflections)
- D_6: regular hexagon (6 rotations, 6 reflections)
declarations.title: DihedralSymmetry

declarations.comments: Create dihedral symmetry D_n
declarations.title: dihedralSymmetry

declarations.comments: D_2: Rectangle symmetry (2 rotations, 2 reflections)
declarations.title: dihedral2

declarations.comments: D_3: Equilateral triangle symmetry
declarations.title: dihedral3

declarations.comments: D_4: Square symmetry
declarations.title: dihedral4

declarations.comments: D_5: Regular pentagon symmetry
declarations.title: dihedral5

declarations.comments: D_6: Regular hexagon symmetry
declarations.title: dihedral6

declarations.comments: D_8: Regular octagon symmetry
declarations.title: dihedral8

declarations.comments: Get the fold count of dihedral symmetry
declarations.title: dihedralFoldCount

declarations.comments: Get the number of reflection axes in dihedral symmetry
declarations.title: dihedralReflectionCount

declarations.comments:
Translational symmetry: periodic repetition.

A shape has translational symmetry if it looks the same when shifted
by a fixed distance. This is the foundation of:
- Patterns and tiles
- Repeating backgrounds
- Infinite scroll illusions
declarations.title: TranslationalSymmetry

declarations.comments: Create translational symmetry with given periods
declarations.title: translationalSymmetry

declarations.comments: Horizontal periodic symmetry only
declarations.title: periodicX

declarations.comments: Vertical periodic symmetry only
declarations.title: periodicY

declarations.comments: Both horizontal and vertical periodic symmetry
declarations.title: periodicXY

declarations.comments: Alias for periodicXY (lattice terminology)
declarations.title: latticeSymmetry

declarations.comments: Get the X period (0 means no X symmetry)
declarations.title: periodX

declarations.comments: Get the Y period (0 means no Y symmetry)
declarations.title: periodY

declarations.comments: Check if shape has any translational symmetry
declarations.title: hasTranslationalSymmetry

declarations.comments:
Glide reflection: reflection + translation along the axis.

A glide reflection combines:
1. Reflection across an axis
2. Translation along that axis

Common in: footprints, frieze patterns, DNA helix
declarations.title: GlideReflection

declarations.comments: Create a glide reflection
declarations.title: glideReflection

declarations.comments: Horizontal glide reflection
declarations.title: horizontalGlide

declarations.comments: Vertical glide reflection
declarations.title: verticalGlide

declarations.comments: Get the axis of a glide reflection
declarations.title: glideAxis

declarations.comments: Get the distance of a glide reflection
declarations.title: glideDistance

declarations.comments:
Combined symmetry group for a shape.

A shape can have multiple types of symmetry simultaneously.
This type captures all symmetries present.
declarations.title: SymmetryGroup

declarations.comments: No symmetry at all
declarations.title: noSymmetry

declarations.comments: Identity symmetry (trivial, all shapes have this)
declarations.title: identitySymmetry

declarations.comments: Reflection symmetry only
declarations.title: reflectionOnly

declarations.comments: Rotational symmetry only
declarations.title: rotationOnly

declarations.comments: Dihedral symmetry only
declarations.title: dihedralOnly

declarations.comments: Translational symmetry only
declarations.title: translationOnly

declarations.comments: Full symmetry group with all components
declarations.title: fullSymmetry

declarations.comments: Check if group has reflection symmetry
declarations.title: hasReflection

declarations.comments: Check if group has rotational symmetry
declarations.title: hasRotation

declarations.comments: Check if group has dihedral symmetry
declarations.title: hasDihedral

declarations.comments: Check if group has translational symmetry
declarations.title: hasTranslation

declarations.comments: Check if group has glide reflection
declarations.title: hasGlide

declarations.comments: Combine two symmetry groups (union of symmetries)
declarations.title: combineSymmetry

declarations.comments:
Chirality: handedness of a shape.

A chiral shape cannot be superimposed on its mirror image.
An achiral shape has reflection symmetry.
declarations.title: Chirality

declarations.comments: Check if a shape is chiral (no reflection symmetry)
declarations.title: isChiral

declarations.comments: Check if a shape is achiral (has reflection symmetry)
declarations.title: isAchiral

declarations.comments: Determine chirality from symmetry group
declarations.title: chiralityOf

declarations.comments: Symmetry operations that can be applied to shapes.
declarations.title: SymmetryOp

declarations.comments: Identity operation (do nothing)
declarations.title: identityOp

declarations.comments: Reflection operation
declarations.title: reflectOp

declarations.comments: Rotation operation
declarations.title: rotateOp

declarations.comments: Translation operation
declarations.title: translateOp

declarations.comments: Glide reflection operation
declarations.title: glideOp

declarations.comments: Compose two operations (apply b first, then a)
declarations.title: composeOp

declarations.comments: Get the inverse operation
declarations.title: inverseOp

declarations.comments: Convert operation to descriptive string
declarations.title: opToString

declarations.comments:
2D Point groups (symmetry groups that fix a point).

These are the finite symmetry groups in 2D:
- C_n: Cyclic group (n-fold rotation only)
- D_n: Dihedral group (n-fold rotation + reflections)
declarations.title: PointGroup

declarations.comments: Get the name of a point group
declarations.title: pointGroupName

declarations.comments: Get the order (number of elements) of a point group
declarations.title: pointGroupOrder

declarations.comments: Check if point group is cyclic
declarations.title: isCyclic

declarations.comments: Check if point group is dihedral
declarations.title: isDihedral

declarations.comments:
The 17 wallpaper groups (2D crystallographic groups).

These are all possible ways to tile the plane with symmetry.
Each group combines rotational, reflectional, and translational symmetry.
declarations.title: WallpaperGroup

declarations.comments: Get the standard crystallographic name
declarations.title: wallpaperGroupName

declarations.comments: Get the IUCr number (1-17)
declarations.title: wallpaperGroupNumber

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Geometry.Symmetry
